exercises.title: NTT Implementation Comparison
exercises.description: Implement and compare different NTT algorithms.
exercises.starterCode:
import time
from typing import List

# Parameters similar to Kyber
Q = 3329  # Prime modulus
N = 256   # Polynomial degree

# Primitive root for NTT
# psi^2 is a primitive N-th root of unity mod Q
PSI = 17

def compute_twiddles(n: int, q: int, psi: int) -> List[int]:
    """Pre-compute twiddle factors."""
    return [pow(psi, i, q) for i in range(n)]

def bit_reverse(x: int, bits: int) -> int:
    """Bit-reverse an integer."""
    result = 0
    for i in range(bits):
        result = (result << 1) | (x & 1)
        x >>= 1
    return result

def naive_poly_mul(a: List[int], b: List[int], q: int) -> List[int]:
    """
    Naive polynomial multiplication mod X^n + 1.
    Complexity: O(n²)
    """
    n = len(a)
    result = [0] * n
    
    for i in range(n):
        for j in range(n):
            idx = (i + j) % n
            sign = 1 if (i + j) < n else -1
            result[idx] = (result[idx] + sign * a[i] * b[j]) % q
    
    return result

def ntt_forward(a: List[int], q: int, twiddles: List[int]) -> List[int]:
    """
    Forward NTT using Cooley-Tukey butterfly.
    Complexity: O(n log n)
    """
    n = len(a)
    result = a.copy()
    
    # TODO: Implement Cooley-Tukey NTT
    # Hint: Use iterative approach with nested loops
    # Layer by layer, doubling the stride each time
    
    return result

def ntt_inverse(a: List[int], q: int, twiddles: List[int]) -> List[int]:
    """
    Inverse NTT.
    """
    n = len(a)
    # TODO: Implement inverse NTT
    # Hint: Similar to forward but with inverse twiddles
    # and multiplication by n^(-1) mod q at the end
    
    return a.copy()

def ntt_poly_mul(a: List[int], b: List[int], q: int, 
                  twiddles: List[int]) -> List[int]:
    """
    Polynomial multiplication using NTT.
    """
    # Forward NTT
    a_ntt = ntt_forward(a, q, twiddles)
    b_ntt = ntt_forward(b, q, twiddles)
    
    # Point-wise multiplication
    c_ntt = [(a_ntt[i] * b_ntt[i]) % q for i in range(len(a))]
    
    # Inverse NTT
    return ntt_inverse(c_ntt, q, twiddles)

# Benchmark
def benchmark():
    twiddles = compute_twiddles(N, Q, PSI)
    
    # Test polynomials
    a = [i % Q for i in range(N)]
    b = [(2 * i) % Q for i in range(N)]
    
    print(f"Benchmarking polynomial multiplication (n={N})")
    print("=" * 50)
    
    # Naive
    start = time.perf_counter()
    for _ in range(10):
        naive_result = naive_poly_mul(a, b, Q)
    naive_time = (time.perf_counter() - start) / 10
    print(f"Naive O(n²):    {naive_time*1000:.3f} ms")
    
    # NTT (once implemented)
    start = time.perf_counter()
    for _ in range(100):
        ntt_result = ntt_poly_mul(a, b, Q, twiddles)
    ntt_time = (time.perf_counter() - start) / 100
    print(f"NTT O(n log n): {ntt_time*1000:.3f} ms")
    
    print(f"Speedup: {naive_time/ntt_time:.1f}x")

benchmark()
exercises.expectedOutput: Working NTT implementation with performance comparison
hints: Use modular arithmetic for all operations
Inverse NTT needs n^(-1) mod q scaling
Theoretical speedup is n/log(n) ≈ 32x for n=256
